hero.headline: Sharda Online MBA in Strategic Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A+ | NIRF #87 University | UGC-DEB Approved | Total all-in ₹1,54,500

sections.tldr: Sharda Online MBA in Strategic HRM covers Advanced HR Systems, Performance and Compensation Management, Talent Acquisition and Retention, HR Accounting and HRIS, Strategic HR Leadership, Strategic HR Planning and Analytics, International HRM, Leadership and Change Management. Separate from regular HRM spec. Includes Live Industry Project + Capstone Project (double project depth). Total ₹1,54,500 all-in. No scholarships. No bundled perks. NAAC A+.

about.body:
Sharda University offers Strategic Human Resource Management as a separate specialisation alongside regular HRM. This is rare in the online MBA market. Most universities offer a single HR spec. Sharda splits HR into two tracks: operational HRM (talent acquisition, compensation, industrial relations) and strategic HRM (HR leadership, HR planning and analytics, HRIS systems, change management).

The Strategic HRM spec has a different Sem 3-4 structure from regular HRM. Sem 3 replaces the standard common subjects (Entrepreneurship, Production/Operations) with Advanced HR Systems and HR Accounting/HRIS. It includes a Live Industry Project instead of the Cross Functional Elective. Sem 4 replaces Strategic Management with Strategic HR Leadership and adds a Capstone Project alongside the spec subjects. This double project structure (Live Industry Project in Sem 3 + Capstone in Sem 4) provides more experiential depth than any competing online HR MBA.

Sharda University, Greater Noida holds NAAC A+ and NIRF #87 University rank. UGC-DEB approved. At ₹1,54,500 all-in, it is mid-tier. No scholarships and no bundled perks.

EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Strategic Human Resource Management specialisation subjects. Note: this spec has a different structure from regular HRM, with Advanced HR Systems replacing Entrepreneurship and Live Industry Project replacing Cross Functional Elective.

subjects.name: Advanced HR Systems
subjects.desc: HR technology platforms, HRMS architecture, cloud-based HR systems, employee self-service portals, and HR automation frameworks.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, inventory management, quality control, lean principles, and operations strategy.

subjects.name: Performance and Compensation Management
subjects.desc: Performance appraisal systems (OKR, balanced scorecard, 360-degree), compensation structure design, variable pay, ESOPs, and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, gratuity).

subjects.name: Talent Acquisition and Retention
subjects.desc: Recruitment strategy design, employer branding, ATS systems, candidate experience, onboarding, retention analytics, and exit management.

subjects.name: HR Accounting and HRIS System
subjects.desc: HR cost accounting, human capital ROI measurement, HRIS implementation lifecycle, HR dashboards, and people analytics data architecture.

subjects.name: Live Industry Project
subjects.desc: Real-world HR project with an industry partner. Applied learning covering HR problem diagnosis, solution design, and stakeholder presentation.

subjects.name: Strategic HR Leadership
subjects.desc: CHRO-track competencies, board-level HR strategy presentation, HR transformation roadmaps, and workforce planning at C-suite level.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Corporate strategy, competitive advantage, strategic planning, and business model analysis.

subjects.name: Strategic HR Planning and Analytics
subjects.desc: Workforce planning models, predictive attrition analytics, succession pipeline design, HR scorecard, and data-driven talent decisions.

subjects.name: International HRM
subjects.desc: Cross-cultural HR management, expatriate assignment management, global mobility, international labour law basics, and MNC HR policy alignment.

subjects.name: Leadership and Change Management
subjects.desc: Leadership styles, transformational leadership, organisational change models (Kotter, ADKAR), resistance management, and culture transformation programmes.

subjects.name: Capstone Project
subjects.desc: Comprehensive strategic HR project integrating Sem 1-4 learning. Faculty-supervised with external evaluation.

whoHires.body:
Sharda MBA Strategic HRM graduates target senior HR and HR strategy roles:

**Industries:** IT services, manufacturing, banking, FMCG, consulting (HR advisory), healthcare, retail, and MNC India operations

**Entry-level roles:** HR Business Partner (junior), Talent Acquisition Specialist, Compensation Analyst, HRIS Analyst, People Analytics Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Senior HRBP, HR Strategy Manager, Head of Talent Acquisition, Compensation and Benefits Manager, HR Analytics Manager, HRIS Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** CHRO, VP HR, Director of People Strategy, Head of HR Transformation, Chief People Officer, Head of Global HR

**Employers:** IT (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL HR strategy teams), consulting (KPMG People Advisory, Deloitte Human Capital, Aon), manufacturing (Tata, Mahindra, L&T HR), banking (HDFC, ICICI HR), and MNCs (Unilever, P&G, Nestle HR India).

**Note:** Sharda provides career support. Greater Noida location offers NCR recruiter access.

skills.body:
The Strategic HRM specialisation builds CHRO-track skills:

**Technical skills:**
- HR Analytics: predictive attrition model interpretation, workforce planning models, HR scorecard design, and people analytics dashboard construction
- HRIS: HRMS platform evaluation, implementation lifecycle management, data migration planning, and HR automation ROI analysis
- Compensation: job evaluation methodology, pay equity audit, variable pay programme design, and total rewards benchmarking
- Strategic Planning: workforce gap analysis, succession pipeline design, HR transformation roadmap, and HR strategy board presentation

**Applied skills:**
- Talent Strategy: employer branding framework, ATS optimisation, candidate experience mapping, and retention analytics
- International HRM: expatriate cost modelling, global mobility programme design, and cross-cultural team management

**Soft skills:** Presenting HR strategy to boards, leading HR transformation programmes, managing sensitive organisational change, coaching senior leaders on people decisions, and facilitating culture change.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Sharda Online MBA Strategic HRM against peer programmes:

**No direct peer with separate Strategic HRM spec:** Most online MBA programmes offer a single HR spec. Sharda is one of the only universities that separates Strategic HRM from regular HRM, with different curriculum, Live Industry Project (instead of Cross Functional Elective), and Capstone Project (instead of Research Project). This dual-track HR offering is a genuine differentiator.

**vs Amity Online MBA HRM:** Amity at approximately ₹2,25,000 with NIRF #22 has a single HR spec covering operational HR broadly. Sharda Strategic HRM at ₹1,54,500 is ₹70,500 cheaper with HRIS, HR Analytics, and HR Leadership as dedicated subjects. Amity wins on brand and alumni network. Sharda wins on fee and strategic HR depth.

**vs DPU Pune Online MBA HRM:** DPU at ₹1,89,400 with NIRF #41 and NAAC A++ has 7 Sem 3 HR subjects including labour laws and DEI depth. Sharda Strategic HRM at ₹1,54,500 has HRIS, HR Accounting, and Live Industry Project. DPU wins on accreditation and HR breadth. Sharda wins on fee and strategic HR focus.

**vs JIIT Online MBA HRM:** JIIT at ₹1,82,000 with mandatory internship and DEI as standalone subject. Sharda at ₹1,54,500 saves ₹27,500 with HRIS and HR Analytics depth. JIIT wins on mandatory internship. Sharda wins on fee and strategic HR positioning.

**vs Sharda's own regular HRM spec:** Regular HRM covers talent acquisition, International HRM, performance management, HR analytics, IR and labour laws, and OD. Strategic HRM covers Advanced HR Systems, HRIS, HR Leadership, Strategic HR Planning, and change management. Choose regular HRM for operational HR roles. Choose Strategic HRM for CHRO-track and HR transformation roles.

fees.body:
The Sharda Online MBA in Strategic HRM has a total all-in cost of ₹1,54,500.

**Fee breakdown:**
- Tuition: ₹1,40,000 (₹35,000 per semester x 4)
- Exam fees: ₹14,000 (₹7,000 per year x 2)
- Registration fee: ₹500 (one-time, same as application fee)
- **Total all-in: ₹1,54,500**

**EMI:** ₹7,375/month. EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm interest structure with admissions.

**Scholarships:** Sharda online MBA does not advertise standing scholarship categories. Check with admissions for any admission-cycle incentives.

**Bundled perks:** None.

Fees listed are indicative. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Sharda each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Strategic Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by Sharda University under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, NIRF #87 University rank, and UGC-DEB approval. Valid for government HR officer recruitment, PSU applications, and private-sector HR strategy roles. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Sharda Online MBA Strategic HRM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Priya K.
items.liked: Strategic HRM as a separate spec from regular HRM is exactly what I needed. The HRIS and HR Analytics subjects are directly applicable to my HR tech role
items.disliked: No scholarships and no perks. At ₹1,54,500, NIU at ₹97,600 is significantly cheaper with a broader spec list
items.body: I chose Sharda Strategic HRM specifically because I wanted the CHRO-track curriculum, not generic HR. The Advanced HR Systems, HRIS, and Strategic HR Planning subjects are directly relevant to my HR technology consulting work. The Live Industry Project in Sem 3 was valuable. At ₹1,54,500 the fee is mid-tier. The limitation is brand recognition. Sharda is known in NCR but not nationally for online MBA.

items.name: Vikram S.
items.liked: Live Industry Project + Capstone Project gives double project depth. Most competing programmes have only one project across 2 years
items.disliked: EMI was not zero-cost in my case. The financing partner charged interest, which was not clearly communicated at admission
items.body: The Strategic HRM curriculum is the strong point. Leadership and Change Management, International HRM, and HR Analytics are all subjects I use in my HRBP role at an IT company. The double project structure (Live Industry Project + Capstone) gave me two portfolio pieces for interviews. The EMI interest structure was a disappointment. Confirm this before enrolling.

items.name: Neha M.
items.liked: HR Accounting and HRIS subject covered HR cost measurement at a depth I haven't seen in other online HR MBAs
items.disliked: Career services for online students are not well-structured. The placement team is primarily set up for campus students
items.body: The curriculum differentiates this from generic HR MBAs. HR Accounting, HRIS, and Strategic HR Planning are subjects that regular HR specs don't cover. The Live Industry Project was logistically manageable since I aligned it with my company. At ₹1,54,500 with no perks and no scholarships, the value comes from curriculum specificity rather than brand or extras.

faqs.question: How is Strategic HRM different from regular HRM at Sharda?
faqs.answer: Different curriculum. Strategic HRM covers Advanced HR Systems, HRIS, HR Accounting, Strategic HR Leadership, and Change Management. Regular HRM covers talent acquisition, IR and labour laws, and OD. Strategic HRM has Live Industry Project + Capstone (double project). Regular HRM has Cross Functional Elective + Research Project.

faqs.question: Is Strategic HRM as a separate spec available at other online MBA universities?
faqs.answer: Rare. Most online MBA programmes offer a single HR spec. Sharda is one of the only universities that separates Strategic HRM from regular HRM with different curriculum and project structure.

faqs.question: What is the total all-in fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,54,500 (₹1,40,000 tuition + ₹14,000 exam + ₹500 registration). EMI from ₹7,375/month through financing partners (confirm interest rate). No scholarships, no bundled perks.

faqs.question: Is EMI zero-cost?
faqs.answer: EMI options are available through financing partners. Zero-cost EMI may not be guaranteed. Confirm the interest rate structure directly with Sharda admissions before committing.

faqs.question: Who should choose Strategic HRM over regular HRM?
faqs.answer: Choose Strategic HRM if targeting CHRO-track, HR transformation, HR technology, or HR strategy consulting roles. Choose regular HRM if targeting operational HR roles (talent acquisition, IR, compensation) at the execution level.

chooseIf: You want a dedicated Strategic HRM spec (rare in online MBA) with HRIS, HR Analytics, and HR Leadership depth.
Live Industry Project + Capstone Project (double project) adds portfolio value for HR strategy interviews.
Your target is CHRO-track, HR transformation, or HR consulting rather than operational HR.
₹1,54,500 all-in with EMI options fits your budget for strategic HR depth.
consider: Brand recognition matters. Amity, NMIMS, and MAHE have stronger MBA brand positioning nationally.
You want operational HR (talent acquisition, IR, labour laws). Sharda's own regular HRM or DPU HRM is more relevant.
No scholarships and no perks. NIU at ₹97,600 or Galgotias at ₹86,400 are significantly cheaper.
EMI interest rate may not be zero-cost. Confirm before committing.
